_meta.source: Wittgenstein Centre Human Capital Data Explorer v2.0 — SSP2 (middle-of-the-road) scenario. Share of population aged 20–39 with at least upper-secondary education.
_meta.field: Percentage points of the 20–39 cohort projected to hold ISCED 3+ (upper-secondary) education, 2025 vs 2035.

MAR.narrative_en: Morocco's youth education base is rising sharply — by 2035, roughly 2 in 3 young Moroccans will hold upper-secondary or higher. Manual-only profiles will face stiffer competition.

IND.narrative_en: India is on track to add ~14 percentage points of secondary-educated youth in a decade. Routine clerical roles will be more contested; durable human-skill profiles stay valuable.

GHA.narrative_en: Ghana's secondary-educated youth share grows from ~44% to ~56% by 2035. Skilled trades remain undersupplied.

KEN.narrative_en: Kenya's secondary-completion is rising fast; technical and ICT-adjacent skills will see the largest premium.
